title: M.Sc. in 3D Immersive Technologies
organization: Arts et Métiers Institute of Technology
description: Master's thesis focused on the development of a VR interface for industrial design prototyping.

title: Research Assistant
description: Research on VR applications for industrial design prototyping.

title: PhD in Robotic Telexistence for Maintenance
description: PhD research on immersive remote maintenance and telexistence. Developed VR interfaces for industrial robot control. Certified in FANUC Teach Pendant Programming (Sept 2021).

publications.title: Key principles for assessing and implementing remote in-spection with telexistence capability
publications.description: Establishes a framework of principles for implementing telexistence in industrial inspection, focusing on human factors and system requirements.

publications.title: Designing Immersive Tools for Expert and Worker Remote Collaboration
publications.description: Explores the design of AR/VR tools that facilitate real-time collaboration between remote experts and on-site workers for maintenance tasks.

publications.title: Telexistence-based remote maintenance for marine engineers
publications.venue: IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces Abstracts and Workshops (VRW)
publications.description: Presents a telexistence system designed to allow marine engineers to perform remote maintenance on ships using immersive interfaces.

publications.title: A telexistence interface for remote control of a physical industrial robot via data distribution service
publications.venue: IFIP International Conference on Product Lifecycle Management
publications.description: Develops a low-latency telexistence interface using DDS to enable precise remote control of physical industrial robots through VR.

publications.title: ImPro: Immersive Prototyping in Virtual Environments for Industrial Designers
publications.description: Introduces 'ImPro', a VR application that allows industrial designers to prototype and evaluate concepts in a fully immersive digital environment.

title: Lecturer
highlights: Course Design: Created and taught the Human-Computer Interaction syllabus.
Teaching: 135h per semester. Topics: HCI, General IT, Computer Technology & AI projects.
Research outputs: 1 journal article (MDPI Virtual Worlds).

publications.title: Study of Visualization Modalities on Industrial Robot Teleoperation for Inspection in a Virtual Co-Existence Space
publications.description: Evaluates how different visual feedback methods in VR affect operator performance and spatial awareness during remote industrial robot inspection.

title: R&D Research Scientist
description: Engineered a hierarchical Vision-Language-Model (VLM/LLM) digital twin framework to translate natural language prompts into expressive robotic trajectories. Focus on the [5GMetaverse project](https://5gmetaverse.wp.imt.fr/).
